figures.caption: Table 5. Clinical-guidance feature ablation (Classification). Each row reports the change in performance relative to the full clinically guided model (all features enabled). Values are mean ± std over 3 folds; negative values indicate a performance drop. Bold denotes the largest degradation and Underline denotes the second-largest degradation within each dataset/metric column.

Fig. 1. Overview of the proposed clinically guided multitask framework. A shared encoder jointly performs nodule segmentation and TI-RADS classification influenced by clinical guidance, while RLAR regularizes task interaction in the shared representation.
